mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and lower near-term recession risk; higher employment growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point PAYEMS latest vintage value was 159540 for 2025-08
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month growth signal has Rank IC below 0.03 in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows or triggered high-growth months do not exceed baseline 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4% and CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating, equity discount rates remain restrictive and inflation risk premia rise; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth is higher than its prior 3-month value
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point FEDFUNDS latest vintage value was 4.22
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability; when unemployment rises sharply, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point UNRATE latest vintage value was 4.3
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after the trigger are positive in more than 55% of cases or if average triggered returns are not below unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in HOUST signals weaker residential investment, credit sensitivity, and reduced cyclical demand; falling housing starts predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point HOUST latest vintage value was 1307 for 2025-08
failure_condition: Reject if negative HOUST momentum is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or if the return spread versus non-triggered periods is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO momentum indicates rising factory output and operating leverage for cyclical earnings; when industrial production growth turns positive, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive.
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point INDPRO latest vintage value was 103.9203 for 2025-08
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO positive-turn trigger has average 20-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS combined with decelerating CPIAUCSL signals policy easing without an inflation shock; lower discount rates and stable inflation predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change is below -0.25 percentage points and CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth is below its prior 6-month value
failure_condition: Reject if the joint easing-disinflation trigger does not produce higher 60-day returns than FEDFUNDS declines alone or if triggered returns are positive in less than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid RSAFS growth with high CPIAUCSL inflation and restrictive FEDFUNDS identifies demand overheating that invites tighter financial conditions; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds 1.5%, CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3%, and FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point RSAFS latest vintage value was 732010 for 2025-08
failure_condition: Reject if the three-variable overheating trigger has nonnegative average 5-day returns or if removing CPIAUCSL does not reduce predictive performance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Negative GDPC1 growth together with rising UNRATE confirms a broad demand contraction rather than a statistical GDP wobble; this predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 0% and UNRATE is above its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:At the 2025-10-15 decision point GDPC1 latest vintage value was 23770.976 for 2025-04
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1 contraction and elevated UNRATE episodes do not underperform unconditional 60-day returns or if the interaction term is not negative in out-of-sample tests.
